docs.title: Overview
docs.text: ckanext-relationship adds relationship-backed fields to CKAN datasets and a small API for managing those links. It integrates with ckanext-scheming and can optionally expose a sysadmin dashboard.

docs.title: Relationship types
docs.text:
The extension supports three relationship types:

  • related_to creates A related_to B and B related_to A
  • child_of creates A child_of B and B parent_of A
  • parent_of creates A parent_of B and B child_of A

docs.title: What the extension includes
docs.text:
• A related_entity scheming preset for relationship fields.
  • CKAN actions for creating, deleting, and listing relationships.
  • Default form and display snippets for package, organization, and group links.
  • A package autocomplete widget for large package target lists.
  • An optional ckanext-tables dashboard for sysadmins.

docs.title: Entity support
docs.text: Capability Packages Organizations Groups Default relationship field yes yes yes Read-page links yes yes yes Autocomplete widget yes no no Admin dashboard yes yes yes

docs.title: Actions and API
docs.text: This page documents the relationship action API, the public routes registered by the extension, and its package_show integration.

docs.title: relationship_relation_create
docs.text:
Creates a relationship.

Parameters:

Key Required Notes subject_id yes Use the CKAN ID. Entity names are also accepted in the current implementation object_id yes Use the CKAN ID. Entity names are also accepted in the current implementation relation_type yes related_to, child_of, or parent_of extras no Extra metadata stored with the relationship

Behavior:

  • Duplicate relationships are ignored.
  • The reverse relationship is created automatically.

Example:

tk.get_action("relationship_relation_create")(
    {"user": "alice"},
    {
        "subject_id": package_a_id,
        "object_id": package_b_id,
        "relation_type": "related_to",
        "extras": {"source": "manual-link"},
    },
)

docs.title: relationship_relation_delete
docs.text:
Deletes a relationship.

Parameters:

Key Required Notes subject_id yes CKAN ID or entity name object_id yes CKAN ID or entity name relation_type no If omitted, all matching rows between the two entities are removed

Behavior:

  • Removes the relationship in both directions.

docs.title: relationship_relations_list
docs.text:
Returns matching relationship rows for a subject.

Parameters:

Key Required Notes subject_id yes Use the subject entity ID. Name lookups are supported only for some legacy rows object_entity no package, organization, or group object_type no Related entity type relation_type no related_to, child_of, or parent_of

docs.title: relationship_relations_ids_list
docs.text: Same filter contract as relationship_relations_list, but returns a unique list of object_id values.

docs.title: Identifier behavior
docs.text:
For predictable results, use CKAN IDs in all relationship actions.

The current implementation also supports some name-based behavior for backward compatibility:

  • relationship_relation_create accepts entity names as well as IDs
  • relationship_relation_delete can remove rows addressed by ID or by name
  • relationship_relations_list and relationship_relations_ids_list are asymmetric: an ID lookup can find rows created by name, but a name lookup does not reliably find rows created by ID

docs.title: relationship_get_entity_list
docs.text:
Returns entities of a given kind and type.

Parameters:

Key Required entity yes entity_type yes

Return shape:

  • list of (id, name, title) tuples

docs.title: relationship_autocomplete
docs.text:
Package-only autocomplete backend used by the autocomplete form snippet.

Parameters:

Key Required current_entity_id yes entity_type yes incomplete no updatable_only no owned_only no check_sysadmin no format_autocomplete_helper no

Return shape:

  • JSON payload formatted by relationship_format_autocomplete or a custom helper

See Package autocomplete for field-level usage.

docs.title: Optional graph plugin
docs.text: The graph action API and graph endpoint are registered only when the optional relationship_graph plugin is enabled.

docs.title: relationship_graph
docs.text:
Returns a graph-shaped JSON payload for the requested center object.

Parameters:

Key Required Notes object_id yes CKAN ID or legacy name of the graph center object_entity no package, organization, or group. Defaults to package object_type no Defaults to dataset depth no Breadth-first traversal depth, 1..4 relation_types no Filter list of relation types max_nodes no Node cap, 1..300, defaults to 100 include_unresolved no Include unresolved legacy name-based nodes include_reverse no Traverse rows where the current node appears as object_id with_titles no Include human-readable titles when available

Return shape:

{
  "nodes": [],
  "edges": [],
  "meta": {
    "depth": 2,
    "max_nodes": 100,
    "truncated": false
  }
}

Notes:

  • The traversal is breadth-first.
  • Cycles are deduplicated with a visited set.
  • Legacy rows stored by name are supported.
  • If the center object is missing, the action raises NotFound.
  • If the current user cannot read the center object, the action raises NotAuthorized.

docs.title: /api/2/util/relationships/autocomplete
docs.text:
Purpose:

  • Frontend endpoint for the package autocomplete widget.
  • Useful for custom frontends that want the same search behavior.

docs.title: /api/2/util/relationships/graph
docs.text:
Purpose:

  • Frontend endpoint for the relationship graph snippet from the optional relationship_graph plugin.
  • Returns the relationship_graph payload as JSON.

Query parameters:

Parameter Required object_id yes object_entity no object_type no depth no relation_types no max_nodes no include_unresolved no include_reverse no with_titles no

Notes:

  • Returns 404 when the center object cannot be resolved.
  • Returns 403 when the current user cannot read the center object.

docs.title: /relationship/section
docs.text:
Purpose:

  • Fragment endpoint for lazy-loading related packages with the shipped template snippets.

Query parameters:

Parameter Required pkg_id yes object_type yes relation_type yes start no size no

Notes:

  • This route is package-only.
  • The default batch size is 20.

docs.title: package_show chaining
docs.text:
Relationship-backed fields can be included in package_show output as lists of related entity IDs.

Example:

package = tk.get_action("package_show")(
    {"ignore_auth": True},
    {"id": package_id, "with_relationships": True},
)

related_ids = package["related_packages"]

docs.title: Default hiding on search and read
docs.text:
By default, relationship fields are omitted from package_show output on:

  • search
  • read

You can:

  • Change that globally with ckanext.relationship.views_without_relationships_in_package_show.
  • Force inclusion for a specific request by passing with_relationships=True.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: All configuration options are prefixed with ckanext.relationship..

docs.title: ckanext.relationship.show_relationship_graph_on_read
docs.text:
Controls whether the optional relationship graph section is automatically added to dataset read pages when the relationship_graph plugin is enabled.

Type bool Default true

Default effect:

  • dataset read pages render the relationship graph section automatically
  • manual use of relationship_graph/snippets/graph.html is still available

Example:

ckanext.relationship.show_relationship_graph_on_read = false

docs.title: ckanext.relationship.views_without_relationships_in_package_show
docs.text:
Controls which endpoint names omit relationship-backed fields from package_show responses.

Type list Default search read

Default effect:

  • search responses do not include relationship fields
  • read responses do not include relationship fields

To include relationship fields in a specific package_show call, pass:

{"with_relationships": True}

Example:

ckanext.relationship.views_without_relationships_in_package_show = search read

docs.title: ckanext.relationship.async_package_index_rebuild
docs.text:
Controls how related package indexes are rebuilt after relationship changes.

When enabled, the plugin schedules background reindex jobs instead of rebuilding indexes immediately.

Type bool Default false

This setting affects rebuilds triggered by:

  • dataset create and update when relationship-backed fields change
  • dataset deletion

Direct calls to relationship_relation_create and relationship_relation_delete do not trigger package reindexing on their own.

Example:

ckanext.relationship.async_package_index_rebuild = true

docs.title: ckanext.relationship.redis_queue_name
docs.text:
The Redis queue name used when ckanext.relationship.async_package_index_rebuild is enabled.

Type base Default default

Example:

ckanext.relationship.redis_queue_name = relationship

docs.title: Practical recommendations
docs.text:
• Keep the default views_without_relationships_in_package_show unless you explicitly need relationship values on search or read pages.
  • Keep show_relationship_graph_on_read = true unless you want to place the graph manually in custom templates or disable it on read pages entirely.
  • Turn on async index rebuilds on larger sites where synchronous reindexing slows down create or update requests.
  • Use a dedicated queue name when you want to isolate relationship reindex jobs from other CKAN worker traffic.

docs.title: Dashboard
docs.text: The optional dashboard provides a sysadmin-only relationship browser built on top of ckanext-tables.

docs.title: Enable the dashboard
docs.text:
Install the optional dependency:

pip install "ckanext-relationship[dashboard]"

Enable the plugins:

ckan.plugins = ... relationship tables relationship_dashboard ...

If you also use scheming-backed relationship fields, the full stack typically looks like this:

ckan.plugins = ... scheming_datasets relationship tables relationship_dashboard ...

docs.title: Access and route
docs.text:
• URL: /ckan-admin/relationships/dashboard
  • Access: sysadmin only
  • A dashboard link is added for sysadmins in the CKAN interface

docs.title: What the table shows
docs.text:
Columns:

  • Subject
  • Subject type
  • Object
  • Object type
  • Relationship type
  • Created
  • Extras

The Subject and Object columns are rendered as links to the corresponding CKAN entity pages.

For UUID-backed relationships, those links resolve to the expected CKAN entity pages. Legacy name-backed relationships can be ambiguous if different entity types share the same name.

Each logical relationship appears once in the table.

docs.title: Actions
docs.text:
The dashboard supports:

  • row deletion
  • bulk deletion
  • filtering
  • sorting
  • export through the standard ckanext-tables exporters

Large extras values are shown in a compact table-friendly format.

docs.title: Export behavior
docs.text: Exports contain plain text labels rather than HTML links.

docs.title: Graph
docs.text: The optional graph plugin provides a Cytoscape-based visualization of relationships for datasets, groups, and organizations.

docs.title: Enable the graph plugin
docs.text:
Enable the base relationship plugin together with relationship_graph:

ckan.plugins = ... relationship relationship_graph scheming_datasets ...

If you want the graph snippet to appear on scheming dataset pages, keep relationship_graph before scheming_datasets so its template override wins.

docs.title: Where it appears
docs.text:
When the plugin is enabled and the dataset type has relationship-backed fields, the graph is rendered as a separate section on the dataset page.

It is not rendered inside the additional_info metadata table.

Automatic insertion on dataset read pages is controlled by ckanext.relationship.show_relationship_graph_on_read, which defaults to true.

docs.title: Graph snippet
docs.text: The reusable snippet is:

docs.title: relationship_graph/snippets/graph.html
docs.text:
Use this snippet when you want to embed the graph manually in a custom template.

Example:

{% snippet 'relationship_graph/snippets/graph.html',
    object_id=pkg.id,
    object_entity='package',
    object_type=pkg.type,
    depth=2,
    max_nodes=80,
    relation_types=['related_to'],
    height='420px' %}

Supported parameters:

  • object_id (required)
  • object_entity (default package)
  • object_type (default dataset)
  • depth (default 1)
  • max_nodes (default 100)
  • relation_types (optional list)
  • height (default 500px)
  • show_controls (default true)
  • show_labels (default false)
  • layout (default cose)
  • include_unresolved (default true)

docs.title: Visualization behavior
docs.text:
• related_to is rendered as a single undirected edge between two nodes
  • child_of and parent_of are rendered as directed edges
  • relation types are distinguished by color and shown in the legend
  • relation labels are shown on hover, not as permanent edge text
  • node labels can be toggled on and off
  • the current dataset is visually highlighted

docs.title: Controls
docs.text:
When controls are enabled, the snippet provides:

  • depth selection
  • fit and reload actions
  • PNG download of the current graph
  • a label visibility toggle

docs.title: API
docs.text:
The plugin also registers:

  • the relationship_graph action
  • the /api/2/util/relationships/graph JSON endpoint

See Actions and API for the full request and response contract.

docs.title: Helpers and Templates
docs.text: ckanext-relationship exposes helper functions and Jinja snippets for form widgets and read-page rendering.

docs.title: Template helpers
docs.text: The plugin registers the following helpers:

docs.title: relationship_get_entity_list(entity, entity_type, entity_query=None, include_private=True)
docs.text:
Returns the available target entities for a relationship field.

Use this helper when you want to build a custom widget with the same target list logic as the default field.

docs.title: relationship_get_current_relations_list(field, data)
docs.text:
Returns the currently selected related IDs for a field.

Use it to pre-populate a custom form or display snippet.

docs.title: relationship_get_selected_json(selected_ids=None)
docs.text:
Returns the JSON payload used to pre-populate the autocomplete widget.

This helper is intended for package autocomplete fields.

docs.title: relationship_get_choices_for_related_entity_field(field, current_entity_id)
docs.text:
Builds the (value, label) pairs for the default select widget.

Use it when you want the same option list as the default relationship widget.

docs.title: relationship_format_autocomplete(packages)
docs.text:
Formats autocomplete results into CKAN’s expected completion JSON shape.

You can replace it by passing another helper name with format_autocomplete_helper.

The following graph helpers are registered only when the optional relationship_graph plugin is enabled.

docs.title: relationship_has_relations(pkg_type)
docs.text: Returns True when the given dataset type has relationship-backed scheming fields.

docs.title: relationship_get_relation_types(pkg_type)
docs.text: Returns the distinct relationship types configured for the given dataset type.

docs.title: relationship_show_graph_on_read()
docs.text: Returns whether the graph section should be added automatically to dataset read pages.

docs.title: scheming/form_snippets/related_entity.html
docs.text:
Default select-based widget for the related_entity preset.

Use it when:

  • you need package, organization, or group relations
  • the option list is reasonably small
  • you want a standard select control

docs.title: scheming/form_snippets/related_entity_with_autocomplete.html
docs.text:
Alternative package-only autocomplete widget.

Use it when:

  • the related package list is large
  • preloading up to 1000 choices is not practical

See Package autocomplete for the full details.

docs.title: scheming/display_snippets/related_entity.html
docs.text:
Read-only rendering for relationship-backed fields.

It renders related entities as links to their CKAN pages.

docs.title: Lazy related-package snippets
docs.text: The extension also ships snippets for progressively loading long related-package lists.

docs.title: snippets/relationship_related_packages_list.html
docs.text:
Use this as the outer snippet for a related-package list.

Example:

{% snippet 'snippets/relationship_related_packages_list.html',
    pkg_id=pkg.id,
    object_type='package-with-relationship',
    relation_type='related_to',
    page_size=20 %}

docs.title: snippets/relationship_related_batch.html
docs.text: This is the follow-up snippet used by /relationship/section.

docs.title: snippets/relationship_spinner.html
docs.text: Simple loading indicator used by the package list snippets.

docs.title: Graph snippet
docs.text:
The reusable graph snippet is relationship_graph/snippets/graph.html.

For full usage, controls, rendering behavior, and screenshots, see Graph.

docs.title: When to use which rendering approach
docs.text: Need Recommended piece Simple schema-backed select widget related_entity.html Large package relationship field related_entity_with_autocomplete.html Simple read-page list of links display_snippets/related_entity.html Infinite-scroll related package cards/list relationship_related_packages_list.html + /relationship/section

docs.title: Practical caveats
docs.text:
• The autocomplete helpers and endpoint are package-only.
  • The HTMX route and batch snippets are package-only.
  • The graph snippet, graph helpers, and /api/2/util/relationships/graph route require the optional relationship_graph plugin.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
ckanext-relationship is intended for CKAN sites that already use ckanext-scheming.

The extension targets CKAN 2.10 and newer.

docs.title: Runtime installation
docs.text:
Install the base extension in your CKAN virtual environment:

pip install ckanext-relationship

If you also want the admin dashboard, install the optional dashboard extra:

pip install "ckanext-relationship[dashboard]"

docs.title: Source installation
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/DataShades/ckanext-relationship.git
cd ckanext-relationship
pip install -e .

For the optional dashboard:

pip install -e ".[dashboard]"

docs.title: Enable CKAN plugins
docs.text:
For the base relationship features, enable relationship and scheming_datasets:

ckan.plugins = ... relationship scheming_datasets ...

For the optional graph UI, also enable relationship_graph.

If you want the graph snippet on scheming dataset pages, keep relationship_graph before scheming_datasets so its template override wins:

ckan.plugins = ... relationship relationship_graph scheming_datasets ...

For the optional dashboard, also enable tables and relationship_dashboard:

ckan.plugins = ... relationship scheming_datasets tables relationship_dashboard ...

docs.title: Run the database migration
docs.text:
Run the extension migration after installation:

ckan -c /etc/ckan/default/ckan.ini db upgrade -p relationship

docs.title: Restart CKAN
docs.text:
Restart CKAN after changing plugins or running migrations. For example, on a typical Ubuntu/Apache deployment:

sudo service apache2 reload

docs.title: Developer installation
docs.text:
To work on the extension locally:

git clone https://github.com/DataShades/ckanext-relationship.git
cd ckanext-relationship
pip install -e ".[docs]"
pip install -r dev-requirements.txt

If you are also working on the dashboard:

pip install -e ".[dashboard,docs]"

docs.title: Building the documentation
docs.text:
Serve the docs locally:

mkdocs serve

Build the static site:

mkdocs build

docs.title: Running tests
docs.text:
Run the test suite from the repository root:

pytest --ckan-ini=test.ini

docs.title: Scheming Fields
docs.text: Use the related_entity preset to add relationship-backed fields to a scheming dataset type.

docs.title: Minimal field example
docs.text:
- field_name: related_packages
  preset: related_entity
  label: Related packages
  related_entity: package
  related_entity_type: package-with-relationship
  relation_type: related_to
  multiple: true

Besides normal scheming keys such as field_name, label, and preset, the relationship field uses a mix of:

  • relationship-specific keys introduced by this extension
  • general scheming field keys that still apply to this preset
  • widget-specific keys used by the shipped form snippets

docs.title: Required relationship options
docs.text: Key Meaning related_entity Target kind: package, organization, or group related_entity_type The type of the related entity relation_type related_to, child_of, or parent_of

docs.title: Optional relationship keys
docs.text:
These keys are introduced by ckanext-relationship itself.

Key Meaning updatable_only Show only entities the current user can update owned_only For package targets, show only packages owned by the current user related_entity_query Custom query or filter for the available choices

docs.title: General scheming field keys
docs.text:
These keys come from ckanext-scheming, not from this extension. They still apply because related_entity is a normal scheming field preset.

Key Meaning required Require at least one related entity multiple Allow selecting more than one related entity form_snippet Override the form widget display_snippet Override the read-only widget form_placeholder Placeholder text used by the active form widget form_attrs Extra HTML attributes for the active form widget

docs.title: Default select widget options
docs.text:
These keys are read by the shipped select-based relationship widget.

Key Meaning select_size Size of the default select widget hidden_from_form Hide the default form control form_select_attrs Extra HTML attributes for the default select widget

docs.title: Package autocomplete options
docs.text:
These keys are only relevant when form_snippet is set to related_entity_with_autocomplete.html.

Key Meaning check_sysadmin Apply owned_only to sysadmins in autocomplete mode format_autocomplete_helper Custom formatter for autocomplete responses

docs.title: Relation types
docs.text: relation_type Meaning related_to Symmetric relationship child_of The current entity is the child parent_of The current entity is the parent

docs.title: Target entities
docs.text:
related_entity can point to:

  • package
  • organization
  • group

Examples:

  • package -> package type project
  • package -> organization type organization
  • group -> group type group

docs.title: Default widget behavior
docs.text:
The preset uses a select-based widget by default.

  • It works for package, organization, and group relations.
  • Existing relations are shown as selected values.
  • The current entity is not offered as a target.
  • If multiple is false and the field is not required, the widget includes a No relation option.

docs.title: Display behavior
docs.text:
The default display snippet renders related entities as links to their CKAN pages.

When to switch to autocomplete

Use the package autocomplete widget when a package relationship field has too many possible targets for a regular select widget.

docs.title: Package autocomplete
docs.text:
Use scheming/form_snippets/related_entity_with_autocomplete.html when a package relationship field has too many possible targets for the default select widget.

The autocomplete widget is package-only. Do not use it for organization or group relations.

docs.title: Example field
docs.text:
- field_name: related_projects
  preset: related_entity
  form_snippet: related_entity_with_autocomplete.html
  label: Related projects
  related_entity: package
  related_entity_type: project
  relation_type: related_to
  multiple: true
  owned_only: false
  updatable_only: true
  form_placeholder: Start typing a project title

docs.title: Useful field options
docs.text: Key Meaning form_placeholder Placeholder text shown before the user starts typing owned_only Limit choices to the current user's packages updatable_only Limit choices to packages the current user can update check_sysadmin Apply owned_only to sysadmins as well format_autocomplete_helper Custom formatter for the JSON response

docs.title: Autocomplete behavior
docs.text:
• Selected values are stored as package IDs.
  • Results are limited to the requested package type.
  • The current package is excluded from the suggestions.
  • owned_only and updatable_only can be combined.

docs.title: owned_only and check_sysadmin
docs.text:
owned_only filters packages by creator_user_id.

For sysadmins, the default behavior is:

  • owned_only=true, check_sysadmin=false -> sysadmin sees all packages
  • owned_only=true, check_sysadmin=true -> sysadmin is filtered the same way as a normal user

docs.title: Public endpoint
docs.text:
The widget uses this endpoint:

/api/2/util/relationships/autocomplete

If you are building a custom frontend against this endpoint, the supported query parameters are:

Parameter Meaning incomplete Search string typed by the user current_entity_id Current package ID, excluded from results entity_type Package type to search updatable_only If true, keep only packages the current user can update owned_only If true, keep only packages created by the current user check_sysadmin If true, apply owned_only even for sysadmins format_autocomplete_helper Helper name used to format the JSON payload

docs.title: When to use autocomplete
docs.text:
Use autocomplete when:

  • the related package type is large
  • loading the full package choice list is too slow
  • you want incremental search instead of a large select widget

Stay with the default select when:

  • the target list is small
  • you need organization or group relations
  • you want the full option list visible immediately
